heading: Configure Atlas for LDAP Authentication
body:
The following procedure enables Atlas to authenticate database users from
OneLogin LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol):

> **Note:** You might incur additional costs when you enable this feature.
> See [Advanced Security](advanced-security).

You may list multiple servers separated by commas. You cannot use different ports.

Add a User to DN mapping similar to the following example to allow
clients to provide their email addresses instead of full DNs when they
connect to Atlas databases:

```sh
[
   {
       "match": "(.+)",
       "substitution": "uid={0},ou=users,dc=<okta_instance_id>,dc=okta,dc=com"
   }
]
```

You may provide self-signed certificates.

Wait for Atlas to deploy your changes. Atlas verifies that
your clusters can connect to, authenticate with, and query your LDAP
servers using the configuration details that you provided.

> **Note:** [Skip this step](configure-okta-authz) if you want to enable
> LDAP authorization.

Add users managed in the Okta LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) to Atlas.

- Click  Add New Database User.

- Click LDAP User.

- Perform one of the following:

  - If you have not entered a User to DN Mapping, enter
    the full DN of the LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) user. Follow this template:

    ```sh
    uid=<user-name>,ou=users,dc=<okta-instance-id>,dc=okta,dc=com
    ```

    For example, if your `<user-name>` is `jane@example.com` and
    your `<okta-instance-id>` is `mdb-example`, your user's
    DN is:

    ```sh
    uid=jane@example.com,ou=users,dc=mdb-example,dc=okta,dc=com
    ```

  - If you entered a User to DN Mapping,
    enter the username or email address that your mapping requires.

- Select the database access level to grant to the user.

- Click Add User.
